ogTitle: Wrathall vs Wrights Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wrathall and Wrights Beach.
heading: Wrathall vs Wrights Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wrathall, New South Wales and Wrights Beach, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wrathall has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Wrights Beach (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wrathall or Wrights Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wrathall has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Wrights Beach scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wrathall scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Wrights Beach scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wrathall or Wrights Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wrathall has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
